items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The city is considering a plan to subdivide a 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This would add 16 residential units to a currently vacant property in the Residential Estate zone.

items.headline: City approves financing for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved lease revenue bonds to fund a new Public Safety Building project on April 28. This major capital project will affect emergency services infrastructure in the city.

items.headline: Council reviews Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 28 to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan will shape how Mountain View manages green spaces and environmental conservation in coming years.

items.headline: Zingsters restaurant opening on University Avenue
items.summary: A new restaurant called Zingsters is moving into 220 University Avenue in downtown Palo Alto's commercial district. This represents new business activity in the downtown core.

items.headline: Mixed-use development with 67 homes and retail planned for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a proposed 4.22-acre mixed-use project featuring 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and 18,535 square feet of retail space including a new two-story building. The project would preserve the existing Speedy's Tacos location while adding significant new housing and commercial space to the city.

items.headline: New dental office opens at The Villages
items.summary: Bay Hill Dentistry is opening a new dental office at 2933 The Villages Parkway in a $629K buildout. This adds healthcare services to the popular residential community.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revising business license tax rates in Campbell's municipal code, with the finance department presenting the annual update. This affects local businesses operating in the city and may impact business costs.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves large stadium signs for tournament
items.summary: The City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on buildings near Levi's Stadium to promote FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This marks a notable visual change to the stadium area during the tournament period.
